---
title: "The coupled pigmentation–hormone network"
subtitle: "The 80-gene selection panel as two coupled molecular modules — the object the dichromatism test is built on"
toc: true
---

Both halves of this project run on **one object**: a curated network that couples the
**pigmentation core** to its physically-linked **sex-hormone axis**. The macroevolutionary arm
tests *this network* for signatures of selection across primates
([the dichromatism finding](dichromatism.qmd)); this page **is** that network — the 80-gene panel
assembled in [NB13](notebooks/13_sex_hormone_layer.ipynb) →
[NB14](notebooks/14_hormone_pigment_interface.ipynb) →
[NB15](notebooks/15_dichromatism_synthesis.ipynb) — shown two ways: as two coupled networks, and
as the signalling pathway that runs through them.

## Two coupled networks

The panel is **27 pigmentation genes** and **53 sex-hormone genes** — two disjoint gene sets that
share no members, joined only where hormones actually reach the melanocyte. Each module is drawn as
its own **STRING v12** network (the hormone side split into its six functional groups as stacked
sub-planes); a thin **interface layer** holds the pigment genes that couple to the hormone axis
(POMC, MITF, TYR, …), and the coupling itself is drawn on top.

```{=html}
<iframe src="interactive/coupled_network_3d.html"
        title="Two coupled 3D networks: the sex-hormone axis over the pigmentation core"
        loading="lazy"
        style="width:100%; height:800px; border:1px solid #e8e2d6; border-radius:10px; background:white;">
</iframe>
```

::: {style="color:#5c656b; font-size:0.95rem; margin-top:0.4rem;"}
**Drag to rotate · click any gene** for a side panel with its full name, role, connections, and the
cited coupling (with PubMed links). The **magenta** links are the three hand-curated, PMID-cited
mechanisms; the **gold** links are STRING functional associations (see *Reading the evidence* below).
:::

## From signal to pigment — the pathway

The same coupling as a directed **hourglass**: four diffusible signals (melanocortin, estrogen,
androgen, endothelin) funnel *down* onto **MITF** at the waist; MITF and SOX10 then fan *out* to the
melanogenesis genes — the curated [Raghunath](notebooks/01_reconstruct_published_network.ipynb)
melanin pathway (TYR, TYRP1, DCT, PMEL, MLANA, OCA2, SLC45A2) — and back down to **melanin**.

```{=html}
<iframe src="interactive/coupled_pathway_3d.html"
        title="From signal to pigment: the hormone-to-melanogenesis pathway in 3D"
        loading="lazy"
        style="width:100%; height:800px; border:1px solid #e8e2d6; border-radius:10px; background:white;">
</iframe>
```

::: {style="color:#5c656b; font-size:0.95rem; margin-top:0.4rem;"}
**Click any node** for its role, mechanism, citation, and signal flow. Thick edges are established;
thin edges are weak or regional.
:::

## Reading the evidence

**Two very different kinds of hormone ↔ pigment link are on screen — don't conflate them.**

- **Cited sex-steroid bridges (magenta / thick).** Three hand-curated, literature-backed mechanisms
  from NB13: **GPER1 → MITF** and **ESR2 → MITF** (estrogen) and **AR → TYR** (androgen). Each is a
  specific *directed* interaction with a PMID and a spelled-out mechanism, and each passed a
  melanocyte-expression gate.
- **STRING coupling (gold / thin).** STRING v12 *functional associations* (score ≥ 0.7) between the
  modules — broad, statistical "these genes co-function" evidence, **not** individually verified
  mechanisms. POMC is the standout hub here: cleaved into α-MSH (→ MC1R, pigment) *and* ACTH
  (→ adrenal steroidogenesis), it wires the melanocortin/pigment axis to the steroid axis.

And the sex-steroid arm is **deliberately hedged**: estrogen via GPER is paper-supported (Natale
2016, +208 % melanin), while ESR2 and the androgen (AR) arm are weak or regional — the panel is a
*hypothesis about coupling*, not a claim of settled human biology.

## Provenance & reproducibility

| Component | Source |
|---|---|
| 80-gene panel (module + functional group) | `comparative-genomics/config/gene_panel.csv` — [NB14](notebooks/14_hormone_pigment_interface.ipynb) / [NB15](notebooks/15_dichromatism_synthesis.ipynb) |
| Cited hormone → pigment bridges (PMID-gated) | `data/processed/nb13_hormone_interlayer_edges.csv` — [NB13](notebooks/13_sex_hormone_layer.ipynb) |
| Within/between-module network edges | STRING v12 · `data/processed/panel_string_edges.csv` |
| Pigment pathway (directed) | Raghunath melanogenesis model · [NB1](notebooks/01_reconstruct_published_network.ipynb) |

Both figures regenerate from the committed tables (no kernel, no network) with
[`scripts/make_coupled_network_3d.py`](https://github.com/tinalasisi/pigmentation-gene-network/blob/main/scripts/make_coupled_network_3d.py)
and [`scripts/make_coupled_pathway_3d.py`](https://github.com/tinalasisi/pigmentation-gene-network/blob/main/scripts/make_coupled_pathway_3d.py).
